_comment: Maps Zoning By-law 569-2013 ZN_ZONE class codes to a per-lot residential unit multiplier. Used by tools/sources/zoning.py (compute_potential for v1.1 neighborhood rollup; lookup_multiplier for v1.2 per-parcel scoring in build_parcels.py). This table is the COMPLETE set of ZN_ZONE codes the ETL recognizes — unknown codes are treated as operator errors and surface as KeyError in lookup_multiplier. When Toronto amends By-law 569-2013 with a new code, extend this file (and the test_zoning_multipliers.py coverage regression). Multiplexes: the 2023 amendment to By-law 569-2013 set the citywide floor at 4 units per residential lot for low-density residential zones (RD/RS/RT/R). RM and RA permit higher densities under their site-specific FSI/height envelopes; values here are conservative averages and are not legally authoritative.

RD.comment: Residential Detached. Multiplex floor of 4 units per By-law 569-2013 amendment (2023).

RS.comment: Residential Semi-detached. Multiplex floor of 4 units per By-law 569-2013 amendment (2023).

RT.comment: Residential Townhouse. Multiplex floor of 4 units per By-law 569-2013 amendment (2023).

R.comment: Residential, generic. Treated as multiplex-eligible per the 2023 amendment.

RM.comment: Residential Multiple Dwelling. Permits low-rise multi-unit (~6-10 units typical for a standard lot under §150).

RA.comment: Residential Apartment. Conservative average across as-of-right RA envelopes; site-specific RA(x...) lots can support far more, but the floor invariant in compute_potential clamps neighborhoods where existing already exceeds this.

RAC.comment: Residential Apartment Commercial. Mixed-use mid-rise; conservative floor between RM (8) and RA (20), discounted from RA for the commercial component on the lower storeys. Site-specific RAC(x...) envelopes vary.

CR.comment: Commercial-Residential mixed-use. Conservative; residential portion only. CR FSI varies widely by site.

CRE.comment: Commercial Residential Employment. Treated as mixed-use multiplex floor.

CL.comment: Commercial Local. Mixed-use small-scale; 4-unit multiplex floor applies to the residential component.
